DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-31-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Tina Farazian, of Strategic Legal Practices, Tel. 310.929.4900, for plaintiffs;
David A. Serrano, of Gordon & Rees LLP, Tel. 415.986.5900, for defendants.

Ruling: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FORD MOTOR COMPANY DEMURRER to the sixth cause of action for fraud is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Mr. Martinez has leave to amend to plead facts to avoid the 3-year statute of limitations of CCP 338. Paragraph 8 of the complaint alleges that Mr. Martinez purchased the vehicle on September 24, 2013 and he filed this action more than 3 years later on November 1, 2016. Mr. Martinez's allegations regarding tolling, his discovery of the harm, concealment, etc. at paragraph 7 of the complaint are totally conclusory. Mr. Martinez needs to allege facts to support his theories to avoid the statute of limitations. (Hobart v. Hobart Estate Co. (1945) 26 Cal.2d 412, 437; Fox v. Ethicon Endo-Surgery, Inc. (2005) 35 Cal.4th 797, 808; McDonald v Antelope Valley Community College Dist. (2008) 45 Cal.4th 88, 102; Baker v. Beech Aircraft Corp. (1974) 39 Cal.App.3d 315, 321.) Ford's remaining arguments have little merit. Less particularity is required when the facts lie within Ford's knowledge. (See Alfaro v. Comm. Housing Imp. Systems & Planning Ass'n (2009) 171 Cal.App.4th 1356, 1384.) Paragraphs 38 and 42-43 of the complaint support a fraud theory of liability based on Ford's exclusive knowledge of the defect/s. "[W]here one party to a transaction has sole knowledge or access to material facts and knows that such facts are not known or reasonably discoverable by the other party, then a duty to disclose exists." (Shapiro v. Sutherland (1998) 64 Cal.App.4th 1534, 1544.) Paragraph 44 of the complaint also alleges a theory of recovery based upon active concealment. Ford can conduct discovery to determine the scope of Mr. Martinez's damages. The economic loss rule does not apply if Mr. Martinez can plead a viable fraud claim. Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Gena Eales, CSR No. 9176, Email: geales@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
